Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple The attackers targeted specific water and wastewater system employees with tailored spear phishing emails and compromised employee workstations through malicious websites or ads.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery, Web, Phishing, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), PowerShell Script, File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers sent tailored spear phishing emails to specific water and wastewater system employees.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromised employee workstations through malicious websites or ads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers used Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for initial access to internal systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers executed malware using Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI).

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attackers utilized PowerShell for executing malicious scripts.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupted SCADA systems to cause power outages.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers deleted critical data in water treatment plants.

Scenario.summary: The attackers targeted water and wastewater systems through spear phishing emails and drive-by compromises, then executed malware using RDP, WMI, and PowerShell, causing denial-of-service attacks and deleting critical data.
